rulings.Case Title: KATHRYN ZUSCHIN VS. THE AMELIA CHAN YEE 2004 LIVING TRUST, BY AND THRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Dismiss Pltf'S Complaint; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 9, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT THE AMELIA CHAN YEE 2004 LIVING TRUST, BY AND THROUGH ITS TRUSTEE, AMELIA CHAN YEE'S MOTICE OF MOITON AND MOTION TO DISMISS COMPLAINT. Granted. This action is dismissed pursuant to CCP 583.310 because plaintiff failed to bring this action to trial within 5 years after it was commenced. The over four years between the entry of default and the entry of the default judgment is not excluded because plaintiff failed to explain why it took her so long to obtain the default judgment. (Hughes v. Kimble (1992) 5 Cal.App.4th 59). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO TIFFANY GARDENS HOMEOWNERS VS. TIFFANY GARDENS LP A CALIFORNIA LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joint Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Setrlement By Defts;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, MAy 9, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT TIFFANY GARDENS, L.P., SAN FRANCISCO TIFFANY GARDENS, LLC's Joint Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Huibonhoa, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. = (302/JPT)

PLAINTIFFS DAVID YANG AND MIMI YANG'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Amendment To Plaintiffs' Third Amended Complaint. Granted conditioned on plaintiffs' agreement that defendants may file and serve a summary judgment and/or adjudication motion directed to the fourth amended compalint to be heard on 30 (rather than the statutorily-required 75) days notice excluding any additional days for service other than by personal delivery with a hearing date of June 29, 2012. rulings.Case Title: MECHANICS BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOHN T. KONTRABECKI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Defendant'S Ex Parte Motion For All Relief Other Than The Stay Of Execution
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 9, 2012, Line 25. Hearing On Defendant'S Ex Parte Motion For All Relief Other Than The Stay Of Execution. Denied. The writ of execution was properly issued after entry of judgment. Granting the relief sought by Mr. Kontrabecki could potentially make the levied funds unavailable for execution. Only in the event that Mr. Kontrabecki posts a bond is it appropriate to return the levied funds to him. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SECTOR 10, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al VS. BANK OF AMERICA CORP. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bank Of America, N.A. Motion To Strike 3rd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 9, 2012, Line 24. Defendant Bank Of America, N.A.'S Motion To Strike 3rd Amended Complaint. Granted. Plaintiffs have not alleged a sufficient basis for attorney's fees or restitution under the Unfair Competition Law. As to fees, Plaintiffs have not shown this action vindicates an important public right, confers a significant benefit on the general public or imposes a financial burden on plaintiffs which is out of proportion to their individual stake. As to restitution, neither Defendant is in possession of funds or has benefited from any property gained through any alleged unfair business practice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TINA SOBERANIS, EXECUTOR OF THE ESTATE OF VS. KATHERINE GRIMME et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Partial Judgment On The Pleadings As To Cause Of Action 2 For Partition
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 9, 2012, Line 9. DEFENDANT KATHERINE GRIMME, AND JOE CASTELLANO'S Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Partial Judgment On The Pleadings As To Cause Of Action 2 For Partition. Granted. Defendants concede that Plaintiff is entitled to an interlocutory judgment for partition. Plaintiff and Defendants Grimme and Castellano are tenants in common and plaintiff owns a 50% undivided interest in 4131-4133 Clement St. and defendants own the remaining 50%. The parties are to meet and confer regarding the appointment of a referee to effectuate the sale. If the parties cannot agree on a referee and sale process, then either side may file another motion. The costs of the partition will come from the sale proceeds. The apportionment of the partition costs will be determined after the sale with the input of the referee. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)
